heading: Possession with intent to sell, and sale, of obscene matter on Federal property
label: 1460 Possession with intent to sell, and sale, of obscene matter on Federal property
notes: Amendments1990—Subsec. (a). , struck out “or a visual depiction of a minor engaging in or assisting another person to engage in sexually explicit conduct,” after “visual depiction” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 101–647, § 323(c)(1)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “For the purposes of this section—Pub. L. 101–647, § 323(c)(2)“(1) the term ‘visual depiction’ includes undeveloped film and videotape but does not include mere words; and“(2) the terms ‘minor’ and ‘sexually explicit conduct’ have the meaning given those terms in chapter 110 of this title.”
amendments: Amendments1990—Subsec. (a). , struck out “or a visual depiction of a minor engaging in or assisting another person to engage in sexually explicit conduct,” after “visual depiction” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 101–647, § 323(c)(1)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “For the purposes of this section—Pub. L. 101–647, § 323(c)(2)“(1) the term ‘visual depiction’ includes undeveloped film and videotape but does not include mere words; and“(2) the terms ‘minor’ and ‘sexually explicit conduct’ have the meaning given those terms in chapter 110 of this title.”

children.text: in the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, or on any land or building owned by, leased to, or otherwise used by or under the control of the Government of the United States; or
in the Indian country as defined in ,section 1151 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the term “visual depiction” includes undeveloped film and videotape but does not include mere words.
